title: Network topology between login nodes and storage
difficulty_justification: Requires reading the network topology spec and extracting the path between login nodes and storage, with correct technical detail.
query_text: Describe the network path and bandwidth available between login nodes and the scratch storage filesystem. What is the effective bandwidth?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Login nodes connect to the scratch storage (Lustre) via the InfiniBand HDR fabric. The path is: login01/02 → ib-leaf switch → ib-core-01/02 (fat-tree) → storage OST nodes. The storage link bandwidth is 200 GB/s aggregate (24 OSTs). Individual login node bandwidth is limited to the HDR100 link rate (100 Gb/s = ~12.5 GB/s per node). The fat-tree topology with 2:1 oversubscription means compute-to-compute bandwidth may be constrained during heavy MPI workloads, but the storage path is typically non-blocking at 200 GB/s.

checkpoints.description: Agent reads the network fabric section of cluster topology.
Agent states 200 GB/s storage link bandwidth.

checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer describes the fat-tree path including core switches
